data.title: CoStar Group GAAP EPS of $0.15 beats by $0.02, revenue of $709M beats by $6M
data.description:
• CoStar Group press release (NASDAQ:CSGP): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.15 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $709M (+10.8% Y/Y) beats by $6M.
  • Shares -4.35%.
  • In February 2025, the Board of Directors of CoStar Group approved a stock repurchase program which authorizes, but does not obligate, the repurchase of up to $500 million of the Company’s common stock. The program has no time limit and can be discontinued at any time at the Company’s discretion.

  • 2025 Outlook

    The Company expects revenue in the range of $2.985 billion to $3.015 billion for the full year of 2025, representing year-over-year growth of approximately 10% at the midpoint of the range. The Company expects revenue for the first quarter of 2025 in the range of $711 million to $716 million, representing revenue growth of approximately 9% year-over-year at the midpoint of the range.

    “We expect adjusted EBITDA for the full year of 2025 in the range of $375 to $405 million, a margin of 13% at the midpoint of the range. For the first quarter of 2025, we expect adjusted EBITDA in the range of $25 million to $35 million,” said Chris Lown, CFO of CoStar Group.

  • Q1 revenue consensus of $729.84M

  • FY25 revenue consensus of $3.08B

data.title: Compass GAAP EPS of -$0.08 in-line, revenue of $1.38B in-line
data.description:
• Compass press release (NYSE:COMP): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.08 in-line.
  • Revenue of $1.38B (+25.5% Y/Y) in-line.

  • Full Year 2025 Outlook:

    • Non-GAAP OPEX of $1.005 billion to $1.030 billion. Included in the range is an increase of 2024 OPEX of 3-4%, $10 million of wrap around OPEX from 2024 M&A and $105 million of OPEX from the Christie's International Real Estate acquisition that closed on January 13, 2025.
    • Free cash flow positive for the full year 2025.

  • Q1 2025 Outlook:

    • Revenue of $1.350 billion to $1.475 billion for Q1.
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $11 million to $25 million for Q1.

data.title: Flowserve Non-GAAP EPS of $0.70 misses by $0.07, revenue of $1.18B misses by $30M
data.description:
• Flowserve press release (NYSE:FLS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.70 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $1.18B (+0.9% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Fourth quarter bookings of $1.2 billion, including $618 million of aftermarket activity
  • Power bookings increased more than 40% year-over-year, with over $110 million in nuclear awards during the fourth quarter
  • Initiated full-year 2025 guidance, including organic sales growth of 3% to 5% and adjusted Earnings Per Share (EPS) of $3.10 to $3.30, which at the midpoint, represents a 22% increase versus full-year 2024 adjusted EPS

data.title: Strategy announces private offering of $2B of convertible senior notes
data.description:
Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) which recently changed its name from MicroStrategy said on Tuesday that it intends to offer $2B aggregate principal amount of its 0% convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering.

Strategy also expects to grant to the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase, for settlement within a period of five business days from, and including, the date on which the notes are first issued, up to an additional $300M aggregate principal amount of notes.

The notes will be senior, unsecured obligations of Strategy. The notes will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount of the notes will not accrete.

The notes will mature on March 1, 2030, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed or converted. Strategy will settle conversions by paying or delivering, as applicable, cash, shares of its class A common stock or a combination of cash and shares of its class A common stock, at Strategy’s election.

The initial conversion rate and other terms of the notes will be determined at the pricing of the offering. Strategy intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes, including the acquisition of bitcoin and for working capital.

Strategy (MSTR) shares were down 0.44% in extended trading.

data.title: Watsco projects A2L transition benefits with 8-10% pricing lift and $1B inventory shift
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Watsco, Inc. (NYSE:WSO) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Albert Nahmad, Chairman and CEO, highlighted a "terrific" quarter with record sales, expanded margins, and record earnings and cash flow. He disclosed a 14% growth in equipment sales and 16% growth in residential products, along with an 11% increase in the dividend to $12 per share.
  • Nahmad stated that the transition to next-generation A2L products is well underway, representing a significant opportunity for market upgrades. He noted the company is transitioning close to $1 billion in inventories to support this shift.
  • The CEO emphasized advancements in technology platforms, including 64,000 active Watsco mobile app users and a 16% increase in e-commerce sales, which now represent 35% of total annual sales ($2.6 billion). The OnCall Air platform generated $1.5 billion in gross merchandise value, a 25% increase year over year.
  • Barry Logan, EVP, explained that "new customer growth" drove much of the quarter’s performance, with the highest contribution from new customers reported in years. He noted that leveraging technology has been instrumental in achieving market share gains.
  • CFO Paul Johnston indicated that gross profit dollars will rise with the A2L transition, but gross profit percentages may initially remain stable due to higher product costs.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates a significant shift to A2L refrigerant products starting in Q2 2025, with a full transition expected by year-end. Johnston noted that the pricing for A2L products is set to increase by 8%-10%, driven by higher manufacturing costs and a requirement for system-wide replacements.
  • Barry Logan remarked on pricing opportunities with A2L products and highlighted the role of technology in optimizing pricing and customer engagement.
  • The company emphasized the continuation of investments in technology and acquisitions to support long-term growth, with a focus on leveraging its financial strength and market position.

Financial Results

  • Watsco achieved record Q4 sales, with equipment sales up 14% and residential product sales rising by 16%. E-commerce sales increased by 16%, reaching $2.6 billion annually.
  • The company reported strong cash flow and emphasized its ability to invest in market opportunities, including its 70th acquisition since 1989, completed in January 2025.
  • Management noted that gross margin expansion remains a long-term focus, with aspirations to achieve a 30% gross profit margin through product and service innovation.

Q&A

  • David Manthey, Baird: Asked about unit growth and pricing dynamics for A2L products. Paul Johnston responded that unit growth in Q4 was primarily driven by 410A products, with A2L transitions expected to ramp up in the second quarter of 2025. Pricing for A2L products is expected to see 8%-10% increases.
  • Tommy Moll, Stephens: Questioned the timing of inventory transitions to A2L. Johnston confirmed that 410A inventory is expected to run down by early Q2 2025, with the new A2L products taking over by year-end.
  • Jeff Hammond, KeyBanc: Inquired about market share gains and inventory strategy. Logan noted that new customer acquisitions were the main driver of share growth, supported by technology and inventory investments.
  • Ryan Merkel, William Blair: Asked about pricing for system-wide replacements under A2L. Johnston explained that the transition will drive higher revenue and gross profit dollars due to the need for full system replacements.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were cautiously optimistic, with questions focusing on pricing dynamics, inventory transitions, and margin impacts. There was a focus on potential risks associated with the A2L transition and its pricing implications.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing their strong financial position, technological advancements, and strategic initiatives. Johnston repeatedly affirmed the company’s preparedness for A2L transitions and highlighted long-term opportunities.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management showed increased confidence in market share gains and their readiness for regulatory-driven product transitions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Q4 call showed an increased focus on the A2L transition compared to Q3, with more detailed discussions on inventory shifts and pricing strategies.
  • Management’s optimism regarding new customer growth and technology adoption was stronger in Q4, with specific metrics showing significant gains in user engagement and e-commerce performance.
  • Analysts’ concerns shifted from hurricane impacts in Q3 to the implications of A2L pricing and system-wide replacements in Q4.
  • Gross margin discussions remained consistent, with aspirations for long-term expansion to 30%, but Q4 included acknowledgment of initial stability due to higher costs.

Risks and Concerns

  • A potential challenge is the higher upfront cost of A2L products, which may create sticker shock for consumers and affect demand. Management is addressing this with customer training and enhanced digital platforms.
  • There may be risks related to inventory management and the timing of product transitions, as the company balances 410A sell-through with A2L ramp-up.
  • Analysts raised concerns about broader market dynamics, including the impact of tariffs and potential supply chain disruptions.

Final Takeaway

Watsco concluded 2024 with strong financial performance and a focus on the A2L product transition, supported by its robust technology infrastructure and market position. Management emphasized the long-term growth potential through pricing optimization, new customer acquisitions, and technology-driven competitive advantages. The company remains well-positioned to leverage regulatory changes and expand market share in 2025 and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
